name: Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) — circular desde Arroyomolinos
region_intro: Jogging around Arroyomolinos offers a variety of routes through diverse landscapes. The region features trails that traverse areas with notable elevation changes, such as those found along the Ruta de los Molinos, as well as flatter paths near water bodies like the Cornalvo Reservoir and the Canal de Orellana. These routes provide opportunities to experience the local terrain, which includes open countryside and areas with historical significance. The network of trails caters to different fitness levels, primarily featuring moderate to difficult options. Best jogging routes around Arroyomolinos The most popular jogging route is Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) — circular desde Arroyomolinos, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, offering a challenging run. Another top favourite among local runners is Montánchez - Arroyomolinos – circular via the Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27), a difficult 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This route also follows the historic Ruta de los Molinos, providing varied terrain and scenic views. Local runners also love the Cornalvo Reservoir Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through the area around the Cornalvo Reservoir, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Arroyomolinos is defined by routes near reservoirs and canals, alongside paths with notable elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate to difficult running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Arroyomolinos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I expect around Arroyomolinos?
A: The Arroyomolinos region offers a diverse range of running routes, primarily featuring moderate to difficult options. You'll find trails with notable elevation changes, such as those along the historic Ruta de los Molinos, as well as flatter paths near water bodies like the Cornalvo Reservoir and the Canal de Orellana. The landscape includes open countryside and areas with historical significance.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Arroyomolinos?
A: While Arroyomolinos is known for its more challenging terrain, with 10 difficult and 6 moderate routes, there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in this guide. However, some moderate routes, particularly those around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer less elevation gain and might be suitable for runners looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Arroyomolinos area?
A: Yes, Arroyomolinos features several excellent circular running routes. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280695310/ruta-de-los-molinos-pr-cc-27-circular-desde-arroyomolinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) — circular desde Arroyomolinos&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer, difficult loop around the reservoir.

Q: What are some longer distance running options near Arroyomolinos?
A: For those seeking longer runs, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.4-mile (18.3 km) route. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284530340/canal-de-orellana-circular-desde-torrefresneda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Orellana — circular desde Torrefresneda&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 9.5-mile (15.3 km) trail.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while running in Arroyomolinos?
A: Many running routes in the area pass by notable attractions. You can explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/745456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez Castle&lt;/a&gt; or discover natural beauty at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/722575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) – Sierra de Montánchez y Tamuja&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views and historical context.

Q: Are there any running trails that feature water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by routes near reservoirs and canals. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through the area around the Cornalvo Reservoir. You can also find routes along the Canal de Orellana, offering flat, scenic paths beside the water.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Arroyomolinos?
A: Over 80 runners have used komoot to explore Arroyomolinos's varied terrain. While specific ratings are not available, the community frequently praises the diverse landscapes, from challenging climbs to serene reservoir paths, and the opportunity to experience both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Arroyomolinos area?
A: There are 16 dedicated running routes detailed in this guide for the Arroyomolinos area. These routes cater to various preferences, with a focus on moderate to difficult terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that combine historical interest with running?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991216537/montanchez-arroyomolinos-circular-via-the-ruta-de-los-molinos-pr-cc-27" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez - Arroyomolinos – circular via the Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This route follows the historic Ruta de los Molinos, offering varied terrain and scenic views that connect you with the region's past.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on running trails in Arroyomolinos?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280695310/ruta-de-los-molinos-pr-cc-27-circular-desde-arroyomolinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Molinos (PR-CC 27) — circular desde Arroyomolinos&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial elevation gains of around 390 meters, providing a challenging workout. Flatter routes, such as those near the Cornalvo Reservoir, have much less elevation change, sometimes as little as 30-100 meters.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: Given that the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, with no 'easy' classifications, some of the shorter moderate options might be considered. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.7 miles (7.5 km) with minimal elevation, could be a more manageable option for active families, though it's still classified as moderate.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the running trails?
A: The terrain around Arroyomolinos is quite varied. You'll encounter paths through open countryside, areas with significant elevation changes, and trails alongside water bodies like reservoirs and canals. Expect a mix of natural surfaces, which can range from dirt paths to potentially rocky sections, especially on the more difficult routes.
tips: This picturesque approved route (PR-CC 27) runs through the interior of the Sierra de Montánchez and Tamuja region through a very picturesque landscape of chestnut trees. It has no technical difficulty and can be started from Montánchez or Arroyomolinos.
The origins of the castle of Montánchez date back to Roman times (it began to be built at the time of the emperor Caracalla)
